 Hero/"Something interesting at top" - tagline "See every side of every news story" with subcopy about multiple perspectives and media bias

- A personalized "My News Bias" widget is included.
- Topic verticals such as Local, Israel-Gaza, and US Politics each have a hero section, blindspots subsection, latest stories, and a "Read more" link to dedicated topic pages.
- The layout uses StickyScrollDashboard, topic chips, FeedHero, and InfiniteFeed components.
- The Weekly Briefing is presented as a separate page.

<tool-use data-tool-type="unknown" data-tool-name="create_plan">
<details>
<summary>Tool use: **create_plan**</summary>


Parameters:

```json
{
  "name": "Ground News Homepage MD",
  "overview": "Document Ground News’ homepage information architecture and UX patterns in a reusable markdown spec, then map those patterns to PaperBites’ desired order: compelling hero → weekly briefing → top papers, aligned with your existing [page.tsx](apps/web/app/page.tsx) and [weekly/page.tsx](apps/web/app/weekly/page.tsx).",
  "plan": "# Ground News homepage analysis and PaperBites blueprint (MD deliverable)\n\n## What Ground News does at the top (“something interesting”)\n\nFrom the live homepage ([ground.news](https://ground.news)), the **above-the-fold** experience is not a single block—it stacks **three persuasive layers**:\n\n1. **Value proposition strip** — Headline (“See every side of every news story”) plus one sentence on *why* the product exists (multiple perspectives, bias awareness). This sets expectations before any content.\n\n2. **Featured story / “As featured on”** — One **dominant** story with strong visual and headline, paired with **credibility signals**: outlet names, geography, and their signature **Left / Center / Right** spectrum plus **source count** (e.g. “2 / 613 sources”). The hook is: *one big story + proof of breadth*.\n\n3. **Daily Briefing** — A **compact digest module**: metrics first (“N stories • M articles • Xm read”), a **lead item** with a short explainer, then **teaser bullets** (“+ … and more”) linking to the full briefing page (&#96;/daily-briefing&#96;).\n\n**UX takeaway:** The top is “promise → proof → digest.” Your research product can mirror that as **promise (why PaperBites) → one marquee paper or theme → weekly digest teaser**.\n\n## Middle and lower page (context for structure only)\n\nAfter the hero + briefing, Ground News runs **repeated section templates**:\n\n- **Top News Stories** — Ranked list with **one line of differentiated metadata per row** (their coverage % + “N sources”), not just titles.\n- **Top News (dated)** — Larger cards with imagery and the same L/C/R breakdown.\n- **Topic hubs** (e.g. Israel–Gaza, US Politics) — Each hub: **hero feature**, **“Blindspots”** niche rail, **Latest** list, **“Read more [topic]”** CTA.\n\nFor PaperBites you likely **do not** need topic hubs on v1; your analog is **topic chips + full feed** (already in [apps/web/app/page.tsx](apps/web/app/page.tsx)).\n\n## Mapping to your desired PaperBites order\n\n| Your goal | Ground News analog | PaperBites hook |\n|-----------|-------------------|-----------------|\n| Something very interesting at the top | Value prop + featured story + source/spectrum proof | Keep or elevate [StickyScrollDashboard](apps/web/app/page.tsx) copy + [FeedHero](apps/web/components/feed-hero.tsx) (3-up marquee papers) or **one** hero paper with a stronger “why read” metric (citations, design, recency) |\n| Weekly briefing | Daily Briefing module | Inline **section** teasing [Weekly](apps/web/app/weekly/page.tsx): headline, 1–2 bullet previews, “Read full week” link—backed by &#96;loadWeeklyFeed()&#96; or a small curated subset |\n| Top papers published | Top News Stories list | A **ranked strip or list** (e.g. top 5–10) with **one secondary line each** (journal, year, topic, or “clinical / methods” badge)—distinct from the infinite grid below |\n\nYour current home already has **dashboard → hero (3 cards) → infinite feed**. The blueprint mainly **inserts an explicit “Weekly briefing” band** between dashboard and hero (or between hero and feed) and **tightens the “top papers” story** so it reads like Ground’s “Top News Stories” rather than only the 3-up hero.\n\n## Information flow (reference)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TD\n  promise[Value_prop_and_trust]\n  featured[Featured_story_with_proof]\n  digest[Daily_or_weekly_digest_module]\n  ranked[Ranked_top_list]\n  deep[Topic_rails_or_infinite_feed]\n  promise --\u003e featured --\u003e digest --\u003e ranked --\u003e deep\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Deliverable: single markdown file\n\nAfter you approve this plan, the implementation step is to **add one markdown file** (suggested path: [&#96;docs/ground-news-homepage-blueprint.md&#96;](docs/ground-news-homepage-blueprint.md)—adjust if you prefer another folder). The file will contain:\n\n1. **Executive summary** — Ground News homepage sections in order.\n2. **UX pattern notes** — Scannability, hierarchy, repeated section template, trust signals.\n3. **PaperBites adaptation** — Concrete mapping to StickyScrollDashboard, weekly teaser, top papers list, and existing feed.\n4. **Optional content checklist** — Copy and data you need for each block.\n\nNo app code changes are required unless you later want the home page layout updated to match the blueprint; this deliverable is **documentation first**.\n",
  "todos": [
    {
      "content": "Create docs/ground-news-homepage-blueprint.md with full Ground News IA, UX notes, and PaperBites section mapping (hero → weekly briefing → top papers).",
      "id": "add-md-doc",
      "status": "pending"
    }
  ]
}
```

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here’s what was added — **nothing under `apps/web` was changed**.

### Location

- **[prototype/homepage-demo/index.html](d:\PaperBites\prototype\homepage-demo\index.html)** — single-page static demo  
- **[prototype/homepage-demo/README.md](d:\PaperBites\prototype\homepage-demo\README.md)** — how to open it

### What the prototype shows (top → bottom)

1. **Promise + controls** — Glass panel, value copy, fake **skim mode** pills, horizontal **topic** chips (styled like your app: amber / stone).
2. **Featured papers** — **3-column hero** with gradient placeholders, eyebrow, title, takeaway, “lens” line, read time (mirrors your `FeedHero` idea).
3. **Weekly briefing** — Metrics chips (`18 papers`, `~24 min read`), **lead** story + explainer, **+** teaser bullets, **Read full week** button (no navigation; demo only).
4. **Top papers** — **Numbered list** with a **second line** (journal · year · design · topic).
5. **More from your feed** — Six **card** tiles + disabled “Load more”.

Extras: **Dark/Light** toggle (saved as `pb-proto-theme` in `localStorage`), skip link, “Prototype — no API” in the header.

### How to view

Open `prototype/homepage-demo/index.html` in a browser, or from that folder run `npx --yes serve .` and use the printed URL.

When you want this in the real app, we can port section by section into Next components and hook up `feed-data` without touching files until you say so.

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here’s what was implemented.

### Home ([`apps/web/app/page.tsx`](apps/web/app/page.tsx))

- Loads **`loadWeeklyFeed(selectedTopic)`** in parallel with the main feed and sorts with **`sortPapersByPublishedDesc`**.
- **Weekly briefing** — [`WeeklyBriefingSection`](apps/web/components/weekly-briefing-section.tsx): paper count, total `~N min read`, lead paper + summary, up to two “+” links, **Read full week** → `/weekly`.
- **Top papers this week** — [`TopPapersSection`](apps/web/components/top-papers-section.tsx): numbered rows with journal · year · design · primary topic, linking to each paper.
- **“More from your feed”** heading, then the existing **`InfiniteFeed`**.

### Weekly ([`apps/web/app/weekly/page.tsx`](apps/web/app/weekly/page.tsx))

- Same **dashboard** + skim switcher at the top.
- **Weekly briefing** (with **`onWeeklyPage`**: no duplicate “Read full week”; **“Jump to full list”** → `#all-papers-this-week`).
- **Top papers** again for a consistent, scannable order.
- **“All papers this week”** with the full **`FeedCard`** grid (previous behavior, now clearly labeled).

### Feed helper ([`apps/web/app/feed-data.ts`](apps/web/app/feed-data.ts))

- **`sortPapersByPublishedDesc`** for shared sorting.

### “For you” only when signed in

- **Bottom nav** ([`bottom-nav.tsx`](apps/web/components/bottom-nav.tsx)): **For you** is included only when **`!loading && user`** (hidden while auth is loading so guests don’t briefly see it).
- **Route guard** ([`for-you-auth-gate.tsx`](apps/web/app/for-you/for-you-auth-gate.tsx)): visiting `/for-you` logged out redirects to **`/login?next=/for-you`** (works with your existing [`LoginForm`](apps/web/app/login/login-form.tsx) `next` handling).

Typecheck for `apps/web` passes (`npx tsc --noEmit`).

- Export internal helpers to prevent redundant calls
- Ensure enrichment happens only once per dataset
- Avoid double API requests for the same topic data - Added an `options?` parameter to `loadFeedPage` to skip OpenAlex citation enrichment when `skipCitationEnrich` is true.
- `loadWeeklyFeed` now calls `loadFeedPage` with `skipCitationEnrich` set to true, then enriches the filtered results once.
- The home page was making duplicate API calls by calling `loadFeedPage` twice in parallel:
  - `loadFeedPage(20)`  enriches 20 items
  - `loadWeeklyFeed`  `loadFeedPage(50)`  enriches 50 items
- This caused redundant fetches and inefficient processing due to duplicate OpenAlex requests for the same topic data. 80 enrichments overlapOpenAlex batch dedupes DOIs, so repeated calls may refetch same DOIs, causing inefficiency.

Refactor loadWeeklyFeed to call loadFeedPage directly without redundant loadFeed calls, avoiding duplicate enrichment. This ensures OpenAlex only processes unique DOIs across the combined 20 + 50 papers fetched by home and weekly feed respectively. To avoid duplicate API calls and OpenAlex enrichment, refactor `loadWeeklyFeed` to use `loadFeedPage` directly instead of going through `loadFeed`. This eliminates the need for two separate `loadFeedPage` calls (20 and 50 items), reducing redundant HTTP requests and OpenAlex processing.

- Currently, `loadFeedPage(20)` and `loadFeedPage(50)` are called separately, causing duplicate API calls and enrichment passes.
- A potential solution involves merging these into a single `loadFeedPage(50)` call and splitting the results for hero, grid, and weekly sections.
- This approach reduces redundant OpenAlex requests and improves efficiency by processing unique DOIs only once. For now, keep the current implementation with two OpenAlex batches, accepting potential duplicate DOI requests since fetchCitedByCountsByDoi dedupes within each call but not across separate callsthis is acceptable for v1.

Implement `loadWeeklyFeed` using `loadFeedPage` directly, matching the current behavior with `loadFeed`, and add `withOpenAlexCitations` to the `loadFeedPage` return value. Single paper loading via `loadPaper` uses `withOpenAlexCitation`, which may cause duplication if the list already includes the paperthough this is only relevant for detail pages.

A new UI component `PaperMetaLine` has been created in `components/paper-meta-line.tsx` for use in `FeedCard`, `TopPapers`, and optionally in `feed-hero`.

The `feed-hero` component is client-side and includes a small line under the title.

A `PaperEvidenceMeta` component displays peer review status and citation counts:

- Uses `inferPeerReviewDisplay` to determine peer review status based on article type
- Shows citation count when available
- Displays "No citations" when citation count is zero or null Client components can use `inferPeerReviewDisplay` from shared code since FeedCard is already a client component.

For OpenAlex integration, the `OPENALEX_USER_AGENT` uses `process.env.OPENALEX_MAILTO`, which may not be available in browser bundles. In the browser, Next.js replaces `process.env` at build time, so `OPENALEX_MAILTO` could be undefined. A fallback string is acceptable.

The issue with `process` in `openalex-citations` for client bundles can be resolved by checking `typeof process !== "undefined"` and using a default string when `process.env.OPENALEX_MAILTO` is unavailable. This ensures compatibility in both server and browser environments. The `PaperEvidenceMeta` component displays peer review status and citation counts using `inferPeerReviewDisplay` and handles citation count display with appropriate fallbacks for zero or null values. It supports a compact mode and applies styling based on peer review likelihood. The component is a client-side React component that integrates with shared utilities for peer review inference and citation handling. The code determines peer review status using `inferPeerReviewDisplay` based on article type, then formats citation counts appropriately. If citation data is missing, it shows either a placeholder ("") or dash ("") depending on DOI availability. The component renders peer review status with styled labels and citation count in a flexible layout, adjusting text size based on compact mode. Citation labels now use simpler formatting: "Citations " for missing counts, "No DOI for citations" when no DOI is present, and "142 citations" when a count is available. Loading indicators are removed to avoid confusion, with "" used instead of "" for pending counts. OpenAlex merges async responses, so citation counts are either populated or remain undefined if not found in the index.  
For display, use: `{cites != null ? `${cites.toLocaleString()} citations` : paper.doiUrl ? "Citations unavailable" : ""}`

Sample data in `createSampleFeed` will include fake citation counts using `pseudoCites(id)` for demonstration purposes.  
To avoid complexity, skip OpenAlex enrichment for offline samples and only inject fake data when enrichment yields no result. Sample data generation now includes pseudo citation counts using `pseudoCites(id)` for demonstration purposes. The enrich process in `loadFeedPage` will overwrite these with real OpenAlex data if available; otherwise, `citedByCount` remains undefined. For offline samples, citation data is injected only when enrichment yields no result, ensuring accurate UX representation. If `citedByCount: null` is added to sample papers, the enrich process will populate it when possible, otherwise it stays null. For sample data, include `citedByCount: null` and let enrichment handle the rest.

Update sample data to use peer-friendly article types like "Journal Article" and "Editorial" for varied peer review badges. Currently, article types are limited to "Clinical Study" and "Review", which may not trigger peer review detection via PEER_KEYWORDS. Add a fake indicator for development use only, and skip enrichment for offline samples.

Replace feed-data with enriched data and update `loadWeeklyFeed` to call `loadFeedPage` directly. Use `strReplace` for sample data updates to include proper article types and citation indicators.</details></think>

Updating `feed-data`, adding UI components, and fixing peer-review patterns for sample article types.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
When using Next.js fetch with `next: { revalidate }`, caching may not function properly on the client side. To address this, either remove the fetch cache in the browser or implement conditional logic to handle caching differently based on the environment. When using `fetch` in the client-side infinite feed, avoid using the `next` option to prevent caching issues. Server-side calls should include the `next` option for revalidation, while client-side calls must omit it due to browser restrictions on `User-Agent` headers.

- Remove `next` option when calling OpenAlex from the client
- Set `User-Agent` only for server-side requests
- Browser fetch does not allow custom `User-Agent` headers
- OpenAlex API calls should be made server-side to ensure proper headers are sent When using fetch in a Next.js client environment, do not set the User-Agent header as browsers forbid it. Only set the User-Agent header for server-side requests. For client-side fetch calls, omit the User-Agent header entirely to avoid errors. Infinite feed client-side enrichment works without a custom User-Agent header, though OpenAlex may still respond. Add `loadMore` enrichment after fetching data.

- Remove `next: { revalidate }` from OpenAlex fetch when not available
- Use dynamic request initialization with conditional `next` option
- Set `next.revalidate` to 86400 only for server-side requests
- Omit `next` option for client-side fetch calls</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here’s what was added and how it behaves.

### Citation counts (`citedByCount`)

- **`PaperRecord`** now has optional `citedByCount` ([`packages/shared/src/types.ts`](packages/shared/src/types.ts)); [`sanitizePaperRecord`](packages/shared/src/sanitize.ts) keeps it as a non‑negative integer when present.
- **[`apps/web/lib/openalex-citations.ts`](apps/web/lib/openalex-citations.ts)** pulls **cited-by** numbers from the **OpenAlex** API using each paper’s **DOI** (parsed from `doiUrl`). Batches up to 25 DOIs per request. No API key; optional **`OPENALEX_MAILTO`** is only used in the **server** `User-Agent` (browsers don’t allow setting it).
- Enrichment runs in **[`feed-data.ts`](apps/web/app/feed-data.ts)** after feed, search, related, and single-paper loads, and in **[`infinite-feed.tsx`](apps/web/components/infinite-feed.tsx)** after “load more” so pagination gets counts too.
- If there’s **no DOI**, or OpenAlex has no work for that DOI, the UI shows **“No DOI”** or **“Citations n/a”** / **“Citations unavailable”** as appropriate.

### Peer review signal

- New **[`packages/shared/src/peer-review.ts`](packages/shared/src/peer-review.ts)** exports **`inferPeerReviewDisplay(articleType)`**, which maps PubMed-style **`articleType`** strings to:
  - **Likely peer-reviewed**
  - **Unlikely peer-reviewed** (e.g. editorial, letter, news)
  - **Review unknown**
- This is a **heuristic**, not a database of journal policies. The paper detail page includes a short disclaimer next to the chips.

### Where it shows up

- **[`paper-evidence-meta.tsx`](apps/web/components/paper-evidence-meta.tsx)** — chips + citation line used on **feed cards**, **feed hero**, and **paper detail**.
- **[`top-papers-section.tsx`](apps/web/components/top-papers-section.tsx)** and **[`weekly-briefing-section.tsx`](apps/web/components/weekly-briefing-section.tsx)** — extra line for peer-review + citations where it fits.

### Config

- **[`.env.example`](.env.example)** documents optional `OPENALEX_MAILTO` for polite OpenAlex use on the server.

**Note:** Storing citation counts in Postgres (for faster/offline use) would be a separate follow-up; right now counts are resolved at request time when DOIs exist and OpenAlex returns a match.

---

_**User**_

what is not done from this list yet?

Product ideas
1. Turn Saved into a true “Research Library”
Instead of only:

Saved papers
My papers
Add:

Recently viewed
Liked
Collections or Folders
Continue reading
Because you saved this recommendations
This would make Saved the most habit-forming part of the app.

2. Build a “Why should I care?” workflow
The app already has whyItMatters. Lean harder into this:

show one-line takeaways on cards
add “Clinical impact”, “Method quality”, “Who this is for”
give users skim modes:
clinician mode
researcher mode
founder/investor mode
student mode
That increases usefulness without requiring users to parse every paper equally.

3. Own the author identity angle
Your new “My papers” feature can become a growth loop:

“Claim your profile”
“Track citations / mentions”
“Follow authors”
“See who else publishes in your niche”
“New papers by authors similar to you”
That changes the product from feed reader to professional identity + discovery tool.



UX / UI ideas
1. Put save/like directly on feed cards
Right now the strongest action loop is hidden behind the paper detail page. Add save/like on FeedCard in apps/web/components/feed-card.tsx.

This is high impact because it reduces friction immediately.

2. Make auth feel contextual, not separate
Currently login/signup is functional, but the app should prompt auth when the user hits value:

after first save
after selecting interests
before syncing “My papers”
after liking several papers
That improves conversion because the account becomes a benefit, not a requirement.

3. Improve empty states into guided actions
Examples:

Saved empty state: suggest top papers to save
For you empty state: recommend 3 starter topics
My papers empty state: explain ORCID clearly with one example URL
Search empty state: suggested queries and filters
4. Add trust design
For health/science content, trust is UX:

show source quality and evidence labels more prominently
explain when a summary is AI-generated vs directly supported by metadata
use consistent “Open source / DOI / PubMed” hierarchy


Ranking, peer-review, citations
group/company that sponsored the study



---

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The founder mode label in skim-mode incorrectly displays "Why this matters" instead of "Founder/Investor," but this is acceptable. Feed cards include BookmarkButton and LikeButton and display one-line takeaways with mode-specific lenses like clinical impact or method. The paper detail page features a WhyThisMatters panel showing all four dimensions along with a skim switcher. The saved page is titled "Library" and contains saved papers and My papers with ORCID URLs. - Auth nudge infrastructure includes interests and tryNudge when selecting the first topic.
- Gate modals appear for bookmarking and liking when users are not logged in.
- Partial trust features include evidence badges on cards such as peer review and citations (PaperEvidenceMeta) and OpenAlex citations.
- My papers section uses ORCID/PubMed URL discovery for partial author identity rather than full claim profile.</details></think>

{

  "plan": "# What’s done vs. not (from your lists)\n\nThis is a **read-only audit** of the repo (key files: [&#96;feed-card.tsx&#96;](apps/web/components/feed-card.tsx), [&#96;saved-client.tsx&#96;](apps/web/app/saved/saved-client.tsx), [&#96;for-you-client.tsx&#96;](apps/web/app/for-you/for-you-client.tsx), [&#96;skim-mode.ts&#96;](packages/shared/src/skim-mode.ts), [&#96;paper-why-care-panel.tsx&#96;](apps/web/components/paper-why-care-panel.tsx), [&#96;auth-nudge-context.tsx&#96;](apps/web/lib/auth-nudge-context.tsx), [&#96;bookmark-context.tsx&#96;](apps/web/lib/bookmark-context.tsx)).\n\n---\n\n## Product ideas\n\n### 1. “Research Library” (Saved)\n\n| Idea | Status |\n|------|--------|\n| Saved papers | **Done** — [&#96;SavedClient&#96;](apps/web/app/saved/saved-client.tsx) loads bookmarks and shows &#96;FeedCard&#96; grid; page titled “Library”. |\n| My papers (ORCID / PubMed URLs) | **Done (basic)** — profile URLs + checkbox + &#96;/me/my-papers&#96; when &#96;hasPublications&#96;. |\n| Recently viewed | **Not done** — no route, storage, or UI for history. |\n| Liked (as a library section) | **Not done** — likes exist ([&#96;use-likes&#96;](apps/web/lib/like-context.tsx) / [&#96;LikeButton&#96;](apps/web/components/like-button.tsx)) but Saved page has no “Liked papers” section. |\n| Collections / folders | **Not done** — no data model or UI. |\n| Continue reading | **Not done** — no reading position or resume. |\n| “Because you saved this” recommendations | **Not done** — no reco engine or section. |\n\n### 2. “Why should I care?” workflow\n\n| Idea | Status |\n|------|--------|\n| &#96;whyItMatters&#96; + takeaways | **Done** — [&#96;getTakeaway&#96;](packages/shared/src/skim-mode.ts), cards show “One-line takeaway”; detail has full panel. |\n| Clinical impact / method quality / who it’s for | **Done** — on paper detail [&#96;PaperWhyCarePanel&#96;](apps/web/components/paper-why-care-panel.tsx) shows all four; feed uses **mode lens** (one column) from [&#96;getModeLens&#96;](packages/shared/src/skim-mode.ts). |\n| Skim modes (clinician, researcher, founder, student) | **Done** — [&#96;SKIM_MODES&#96;](packages/shared/src/skim-mode.ts), [&#96;SkimModeSwitcher&#96;](apps/web/components/skim-mode-switcher.tsx), founder maps to “Why this matters” not a separate “investor” label (minor naming). |\n\n### 3. Contextual auth (not a separate wall)\n\n| Trigger | Status |\n|---------|--------|\n| After first topic selection (guest) | **Done** — &#96;tryNudge(\"interests\")&#96; in [&#96;for-you-client.tsx&#96;](apps/web/app/for-you/for-you-client.tsx) when first topic is picked. |\n| After first save / several likes / sync My papers | **Partial** — copy exists in [&#96;auth-value-modal.tsx&#96;](apps/web/components/auth-value-modal.tsx) for &#96;first_save&#96; and &#96;likes_warm&#96;, but **&#96;tryNudge&#96; is not called** from [&#96;bookmark-context.tsx&#96;](apps/web/lib/bookmark-context.tsx) or [&#96;like-context.tsx&#96;](apps/web/lib/like-context.tsx). Guests get &#96;openValueModal(\"gate_bookmark\")&#96; / &#96;gate_like&#96; on click only. |\n| Gate bookmark/like when logged out | **Done** — same modals. |\n\n### 3. <think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The AuthNudgeProvider needs to wrap BookmarkProvider and LikesProvider; the order of app-providers should be verified. For the guest bookmark flow, when adding a bookmark as a guest:
- The `setIds` function checks if the user is not authenticated (`!user`)
- When adding a bookmark for the first time (prev.length === 0, next.length === 1), it triggers a nudge for first save
- A ref is used to prevent unnecessary calls to toggle functions when not a guest
- The `useAuthNudge` hook is added to access user data and trigger nudge functionality
- The setState callback checks if the item wasn't previously saved and if there were no previous items to determine first save

```javascript
setIds((prev) => {
  const wasSaved = prev.includes(id);
  const next = wasSaved ? prev.filter((v) => v !== id) : [id, ...prev];
```</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here are two prompts you can paste into separate agent chats.

---

### Agent A — Research Library

```text
You are working in the PaperBites monorepo (Next.js app in apps/web). Goal: expand “Saved” into a real Research Library.

Scope (implement in order, keep changes focused):
1. Liked papers section: surface likes from existing localStorage + server sync (like-context, /me/likes) on the Saved/Library page (saved-client.tsx) with clear section headings vs “Saved papers”.
2. Recently viewed: persist last N paper IDs (slug or id) in localStorage with a small hook; show “Recently viewed” on Library; record views when user opens a paper detail page.
3. Continue reading (MVP): store scroll position or a simple “last opened at” per paper id in localStorage; show “Continue” on cards or library rows where data exists.
4. Empty states: when Saved or Liked is empty, show 3–6 curated starter papers (reuse feed API or static slugs from sample feed) with “Save” CTA.

Constraints: match existing amber/stone UI, reuse FeedCard where possible, don’t break auth or bottom nav. Update docs/product-ideas-gap-analysis.md table rows if behavior changes.

Deliver: working UI + brief note of files touched. No unrelated refactors.
```

---

### Agent B — Author / growth

```text
You are working in the PaperBites monorepo. Goal: move toward author identity and growth loops (incremental MVP, not a full social network).

Scope (pick what fits one PR; prioritize top items):
1. “Claim / profile” MVP: a dedicated route e.g. /profile or /me that shows email + linked ORCID URLs + “My papers” count; reuse existing /me publication profile data and auth.
2. Optional: public read-only profile slug later—only if time; otherwise stub with “coming soon”.
3. Author discovery stub: a section on For you or Saved suggesting “Add ORCID to see your papers” with link to profile setup; no follow graph yet.
4. Copy: clarify value of account for citations (OpenAlex is per-paper today; set expectations for “your dashboard” as future).

Constraints: reuse services/api routes under /me, don’t add heavy new infra without discussing. Match existing patterns in saved-client and for-you-client.

Deliver: routes/components wired + short summary. Avoid scope creep into follows/similar-authors feeds unless trivial.
```

---

**Tip:** Run **A** and **B** in parallel only if they touch **different files**; if both edit `saved-client.tsx` or `app-providers`, merge one branch first or narrow scope (e.g. Agent A skips “Recently viewed” if B adds `/profile`).

---

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here’s what was added.

### `/profile` — account & author MVP
- **[`apps/web/app/profile/page.tsx`](apps/web/app/profile/page.tsx)** — layout shell.
- **[`apps/web/app/profile/profile-client.tsx`](apps/web/app/profile/profile-client.tsx)** — signed-in only (guests are sent to `/login?next=/profile`):
  - **Email**
  - **ORCID / PubMed URLs** (from `useAuth`, same as `/auth/me`)
  - **My papers** — count from `GET /me/my-papers` when `hasPublications` is on
  - **Citations copy** — per-paper OpenAlex-style counts today; personal dashboard described as future
  - **Public profile** — dashed “coming soon” block
  - Links to **`/saved#author-profile-links`** for editing (see below)

### Library anchor
- **[`apps/web/app/saved/saved-client.tsx`](apps/web/app/saved/saved-client.tsx)** — logged-in **Author profile links** block has `id="author-profile-links"` and `scroll-mt-24` for hash navigation.
- Short line under the Library intro: **Account & author settings** → `/profile`.

### Discovery / growth stubs
- **[`apps/web/app/for-you/for-you-client.tsx`](apps/web/app/for-you/for-you-client.tsx)** — if the user is signed in and has **no** publication setup (no `hasPublications` or no URLs), a **“Surface your own publications”** card appears with **Open profile** and **Add ORCID in Library**.

### Auth chrome
- **[`apps/web/components/auth-bar.tsx`](apps/web/components/auth-bar.tsx)** — signed-in **email** links to **`/profile`**.

`npx tsc --noEmit` passes for `apps/web`. No new API routes; everything uses existing `/auth/me`, `/me/my-papers`, and the publication URLs already on the user object.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
"Failed to fetch" is the standard JavaScript error message for fetch() failures, typically due to network issues or CORS problems.</details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_






_**Agent (composer-2)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **read_file_v2** • Read file: d:\PaperBites\package.json</summary>

</details>
</tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="read_file_v2"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **read_file_v2**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


In PaperBites, **`"Failed to fetch"`** almost always comes from the **browser**, not a custom string in your repo. It means the `fetch()` **never got a normal HTTP response** (the request failed at the network layer).

### Most common causes

1. **API not running**  
   The web app calls **`http://localhost:4000`** by default ([`api-base.ts`](apps/web/lib/api-base.ts)). If nothing is listening there, the browser throws **Failed to fetch** (connection refused).

   - Run the API in another terminal:  
     `npm run dev:api`  
   - Run the Next app:  
     `npm run dev:web`  
   - Confirm in the browser: open `http://localhost:4000/health` — you should see JSON like `{ ok: true, ... }`.

2. **CORS / wrong origin**  
   The API only allows origins listed in **`WEB_ORIGIN`** ([`services/api/src/index.ts`](services/api/src/index.ts)). Default is `http://localhost:3000`.

   If you open the app as **`http://127.0.0.1:3000`** (or another host/port), the browser may block the response and you can still see **Failed to fetch**.

   **Fix:** In `.env` for the API (and restart the API), set:

   ```env
   WEB_ORIGIN=http://localhost:3000,http://127.0.0.1:3000
   ```

   Use the **exact** origin shown in the browser address bar (scheme + host + port).

3. **Wrong API URL**  
   If `NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL` points to the wrong host/port, or you changed ports, fetches go nowhere useful.

   **Fix:** In `apps/web/.env.local` (or your env), set:

   ```env
   NEXT_PUBLIC_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:4000
   ```

   Restart **`dev:web`** after changing env vars.

4. **Database down**  
   If the API **crashes on startup** because `DATABASE_URL` is wrong, nothing listens on 4000 → same **Failed to fetch** when the UI calls the API.

---

### How to narrow it down quickly

1. Open **DevTools → Network** and trigger the action again.  
   - **(failed)** or **(blocked:cors)** on the request to `:4000` → use the fixes above.  
2. Check the **Console** for a line like **“CORS policy”** — that’s fix #2.  
3. Confirm **`http://localhost:4000/health`** loads when the API is supposed to be up.

If you say whether you’re using **`localhost` vs `127.0.0.1`**, and whether **`/health` works**, we can pin it to one of these in one step.

---
